Was Catenaccio purely negative? Think again.

Helenio Herrera's Inter perfected it: a sweeper (libero) behind four defenders, waiting to explode on the counter with devastating speed. Defensive masterclass or pragmatic genius? It won them back-to-back European Cups. That's pure tactical dominance.

Before Messi & Ronaldo, there was King George. George Weah didn't just play for AC Milan, he carried them to Serie A titles. 1995 Ballon d'Or, absolutely crushing defenders with raw power and skill. An African icon who proved individual brilliance conquers all. Pure magic.

Talk about dominance?

Samuel Eto'o is STILL the ONLY player in history to win consecutive continental trebles with two different clubs!

Barça (2009) then Inter (2010).

Built different. A true #9 legend whose trophy cabinet screams louder than most.

Okay, real talk: Was anyone ever more unplayable than R9 pre-injuries?

Ronaldo had two Ballon d'Ors by 24, moving like a cheat code. Seriously, just watch him. But then Thierry Henry spent years shattering records, hitting 4 PL Golden Boots! R9's peak was otherworldly, but Henry's sustained brilliance makes you wonder what could have been. Thoughts? #FootballHistory #LegendaryStrikers

Underrated? Criminal, frankly. Samuel Eto'o is the ONLY player in history to win back-to-back continental trebles with two different clubs (Barcelona 2009, Inter Milan 2010)! Goals, grit, and silverware everywhere he went. A truly iconic striker. #FootballHistory #AfricanLegends
